title: Frontier Queues, Visited Reservation, State Transitions, and Determinism · Goroutine Forensics
summary: A preserved Go crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, transport, body, tokenizer, goquery, frontier, goroutine, Colly callback, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first broken invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in race-safe deterministic frontier state machine and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and e…
objectives: Define discovered, rejected, queued, claimed, fetching, fetched, parsed, retryable, terminal, and canceled states with legal transitions.
Reserve admitted URL keys atomically before enqueue so concurrent discoveries cannot duplicate effects.
Use stable priority and tie keys for owner commitments, depth, page class, freshness, origin fairness, and retry readiness.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Frontier Transition
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go team handles case 6297 during an isolated binary, deployment, and restore defense. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove legal frontier transitions, reserve-on-schedule, stable heap order, one owner, and state invariants; browser Go remains deterministic and free of native network, parser, filesystem, process, and host effects; then change one tokenizer, goquery, selector, domain constructor, record, link, frontier, or trap case and retain rejected evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Frontier Invariants
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crawler-go-frontier-invariants-6boqd7" that uses case 8168 to prove legal frontier transitions, reserve-on-schedule, stable heap order, one owner, and state invariants. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, import x/net/html, goquery, Colly, or robotstxt, access host files, retain credentials, launch processes or unbounded goroutines, or claim native parser, race, load, accessibility, privacy, legal, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, x/net 0.57.0, goquery 1.12.0, Colly 2.3.0, robotstxt 1.1.2, original fixtures, custom RoundTrippers, httptest servers, controlled DNS and browser-service environments, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected binaries and containers, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Frontier Invariants, name the owner, permission, revision, Go, seed, URL, scope, robots, response, body, tree, record, frontier, goroutine, accessibility, report, cleanup, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Frontier Invariants precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Frontier Invariants, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A debug Go team handles case 8107 during a redirect, DNS, body, and checkpoint fault exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove legal frontier transitions, reserve-on-schedule, stable heap order, one owner, and state invariants; failed, canceled, stale, over-budget, and revoked work preserves prior admitted state; then change one content version, metadata, accessibility, resource, browser-service, or report condition without overclaiming. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Check unique active owner, bounded counts, terminal immutability, no lost work, stable ordering, and graph reconciliation.

For Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Frontier Invariants, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Race-detector silence proves logical frontier correctness.

steps.instruction:

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Check unique active owner, bounded counts, terminal immutability, no lost work, stable ordering, and graph reconciliation.
